seoTitle: How to Add HTML or Embed Code in Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to insert custom HTML, iFrames, and third-party embed codes into your Wix website using the Embed Code element.
intro: When Wix's built-in elements aren't enough, its Embed Code component lets you paste raw HTML, external widgets, or third-party scripts directly into your page. This guide covers adding the embed element, pasting your code, and adjusting its container size so it displays properly across devices.

steps.heading: Open your target page
steps.body: Navigate to the section of your Wix page where you want to add the custom code.

steps.heading: Find the Embed Code category
steps.body: From the Add Elements sidebar, locate the Embed Code component category.

steps.heading: Add an HTML iFrame element
steps.body: Deploy the iFrame module and select the Enter Code input option.

steps.heading: Paste your code
steps.body: Insert your custom HTML, CSS, or JavaScript snippet and click Update to render it.

steps.heading: Adjust the container size
steps.body: Set custom width and height boundaries so the embedded content displays without clipping.

steps.heading: Check mobile scaling and test
steps.body: Toggle the mobile editor to recalibrate the container, then preview to confirm the script executes correctly before publishing.

faq.question: Can I embed any third-party widget this way?
faq.answer: Most standard HTML, CSS, and JavaScript widgets work, though some scripts may be restricted by Wix's sandboxed iFrame security settings.

faq.question: Why isn't my embedded script loading fully?
faq.answer: This is often due to security restrictions within the sandboxed iFrame environment, so check whether the tool has known compatibility with embedded contexts.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to use this feature?
faq.answer: You need the actual code snippet you want to embed, but pasting and configuring it inside Wix itself doesn't require writing new code.

faq.question: Will the embedded element resize properly on mobile?
faq.answer: You'll want to check and adjust its container dimensions separately in the mobile editor to prevent clipping on smaller screens.
